Landing.badge: Your first step into vibe coding
Landing.heroTitle: Want to code with AI but
Landing.heroDescription:
Gave up because setting up dev tools was too hard?
VibeStart walks you through it one step at a time.

1.title: A Few Quick Questions
1.description: Just tell us your OS and what you want to build

2.title: Custom Guide
2.description: We'll walk you through the tools you need, step by step

3.title: Copy & Paste
3.description: Copy the commands, run them, and you're done!

Landing.reassurance: No coding experience? No problem. Just follow along at your own pace.

goal.title: Goal
goal.description: What do you want to build?

Onboarding.goalSubQuestion: Which tech stack would you like?

webNextjs.label: Next.js All-in-One
webNextjs.description:
Build your frontend and backend in one framework.
Great for shipping fast and deploying right away.
Used by Airbnb, TikTok Web, and Twitch.

webPython.label: Next.js + Python Backend
webPython.description:
Next.js handles the frontend, Python handles the server.
Perfect for adding AI chatbots or data analysis features.
Used by Instagram, Pinterest, and Netflix.

webJava.label: Next.js + Java Backend
webJava.description:
Next.js handles the frontend, Java handles the server.
Built for handling heavy traffic reliably at scale.
Used by LinkedIn, Uber, and Amazon.

Setup.title: Step-by-Step Setup
Setup.subtitle: Copy each step's commands using the copy button and paste them into your terminal
Setup.claudeMdGuide: Copy the content below and save it as a CLAUDE.md file in your project root.

terminal.title: Open Terminal
terminal.description: Open a window to run commands

detailedGuide.windows: In the Windows search bar (magnifying glass icon), type 'PowerShell' and click 'Windows PowerShell' to open it. After installing the Linux environment, you can search for 'Ubuntu' and open that instead.
detailedGuide.macos: Open Spotlight (Cmd + Space), type 'Terminal', and press Enter.

wsl.title: Install Linux Environment (WSL2)
wsl.description: AI coding tools on Windows require a Linux environment
wsl.whyNeeded: AI coding tools (Claude Code) only run on Linux. WSL2 is a mini Linux computer that runs inside Windows. Once installed, you can use Linux commands while staying on Windows.
wsl.detailedGuide: You'll need to restart your computer after installation. After restarting, an Ubuntu window will open automatically, prompting you to set up a username and password.

troubleshooting.symptom: 'wsl' is not recognized as a command
troubleshooting.solution: You need Windows 10 version 2004 or later. Check your OS build in Settings → System → About, then run Windows Update.
You need to enable Virtualization Technology in your BIOS settings. Press F2 or Del during restart to enter BIOS.
That's fine! Run wsl --update to update to the latest version and move on to the next step.
Search for 'Ubuntu' in Windows search. If it's not there, search for 'Ubuntu' in the Microsoft Store and install it.

wslOpen.title: Open Linux Terminal (Ubuntu)
wslOpen.description: From here on, we'll work in the Linux terminal
wslOpen.whyNeeded: You're entering the Linux environment you just installed. When the prompt (input indicator) changes, you're in the Linux world.
wslOpen.detailedGuide: Enter the command below to switch to the Linux terminal. If the prompt changes, you're in! After switching, type cd ~ to navigate to your home folder.

troubleshooting.symptom: It's asking for a username and password
troubleshooting.solution: That's normal! Enter a username and password of your choice. It's normal for nothing to appear on screen while typing your password.
You may not have restarted after installing WSL. Restart your computer and try again.
Check if the end of the prompt changed to :~$. Type pwd — if it shows /home/yourusername, you're all set.

devTools.title: Install Development Tools
devTools.detailedGuide: This installs multiple tools at once. It may take a few minutes.

whyNeeded.git: Git is a 'time machine' for your code — it saves every change you make.
whyNeeded.nodejs: Node.js is the JavaScript engine needed to build websites.
whyNeeded.python: Python is a widely-used programming language for AI and data analysis.
whyNeeded.java: Java is a reliable programming language used by large-scale services.
whyNeeded.expo: Expo lets you build Android and iOS apps simultaneously from one codebase.

wsl.symptom: sudo asks for a password but nothing appears when I type
wsl.solution: It's normal for nothing to appear on screen while typing your password. Just type your password and press Enter.
Run sudo apt update first, then try again.

wsl.symptom: dpkg lock or another process is using it error
wsl.solution: Another installation is in progress. Wait a minute or two and try again.
Run sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y curl first, then try again.

macos.solution: Homebrew isn't installed. Complete the Homebrew installation step first.
It's already installed — that's fine! Move on to the next step.
Run brew update and try again.

editor.title: Install Code Editor (VS Code)
editor.description: Install a program for editing code
editor.whyNeeded: VS Code is a dedicated code editor. It's like Notepad, but with superpowers — code autocomplete, error highlighting, AI assistant integration, and more.

detailedGuide.windows: VS Code is installed on Windows but automatically connects to WSL. Open a new PowerShell window to run the command.

windows.solution: Search for 'App Installer' in the Microsoft Store and update it. If that doesn't work, download VS Code directly from code.visualstudio.com.
That's fine! VS Code is already installed — move on to the next step.

macos.solution: That's fine! Move on to the next step.
Close and reopen your terminal. If it still doesn't work, revisit the Homebrew installation step.

brew.title: Install Package Manager (Homebrew)
brew.description: A tool that makes it easy to install programs on your Mac
brew.whyNeeded: Homebrew is like a terminal-based App Store for your Mac. You can install and manage dev tools with a single command.
brew.detailedGuide: You may be asked for your password during installation. Enter your Mac login password. After installation, close and reopen your terminal.

troubleshooting.symptom: A Command Line Tools installation popup appears
troubleshooting.solution: That's normal! Click the 'Install' button to proceed. Homebrew installation will continue after it's done.
Enter your Mac login password. It's normal for nothing to appear on screen while typing.

troubleshooting.symptom: brew command doesn't work after installation
troubleshooting.solution: Close and reopen your terminal. If it still doesn't work, run eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)".

aiSetup.title: Install AI Coding Assistant (Claude Code)
aiSetup.description: Install the AI coding assistant and connect your account
aiSetup.whyNeeded: Claude Code is an AI that helps you code through conversation. Say something like "Build me a login page" and it writes the code for you.
aiSetup.detailedGuide: After installation, the login process starts automatically. When a browser window opens, sign in with your Claude account. You can sign up for free if you don't have one.

troubleshooting.solution: Node.js isn't installed. Go back and complete the 'Install Development Tools' step.
Try again with sudo npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code.
Copy the URL shown in your terminal and paste it directly into your browser's address bar.

troubleshooting.symptom: No response in terminal after logging in
troubleshooting.solution: The terminal will automatically detect when you've completed the login in the browser. Just wait a moment.

description.standalone: Set up your very own website project
description.withBackend: Create the Next.js project that handles the frontend

projectFrontend.detailedGuideTemplate: This creates a project in the ~/{path} folder.

troubleshooting.symptom: The application path is not writable error
troubleshooting.solution: Try running it again. The initial download may temporarily fail on the first attempt.
Node.js isn't installed. Go back and complete the 'Install Development Tools' step.
There are leftover files from a previous attempt. Run rm -rf ~/{path} to delete them, then try again.

projectBackendPython.title: Create Backend Project (Python)
projectBackendPython.description: Set up a Python FastAPI server project
projectBackendPython.detailedGuideTemplate: This creates a project in the ~/{projectName}/backend folder.

projectBackendJava.title: Create Backend Project (Java)
projectBackendJava.description: Download a Java Spring Boot server project
projectBackendJava.detailedGuideTemplate: This creates a project in the ~/{projectName}/backend folder. It automatically downloads a project from Spring Initializr with essential libraries for production use.

projectExpo.title: Create Expo Project
projectExpo.description: Set up your very own mobile app project
projectExpo.whyNeeded: Expo is a tool that makes React Native easy to use. You can build Android and iOS apps simultaneously from one codebase.
projectExpo.detailedGuideTemplate: This creates an Expo project in the ~/{projectName} folder. It's TypeScript-based, so you'll be using the same language as web development.

troubleshooting.solution: Node.js isn't installed. Go back and complete the 'Install Development Tools' step.
There are leftover files from a previous attempt. Run rm -rf ~/{projectName} to delete them, then try again.

projectDataAi.title: Set Up Data Analysis Environment
projectDataAi.detailedGuideTemplate: This creates a virtual environment in the ~/{projectName} folder and installs data analysis libraries.

architecture.title: Set Up Project Structure

description.default: Configure architecture rules for the AI tool to follow
description.dataAi: Configure analysis project rules for the AI tool to follow

architecture.detailedGuide: Run the command below to create the folder structure, then save the CLAUDE.md content to your project root.

firstRun.title: First Run!
firstRun.description: Open the project in VS Code and start coding with AI

detailedGuide.simple: The command below opens VS Code. Inside VS Code, open the terminal (Ctrl+` or Cmd+`) and type claude to start AI coding.
detailedGuide.withBackend: The command below opens VS Code. Inside VS Code, open the terminal (Ctrl+` or Cmd+`) and type claude to start AI coding. You'll see both the frontend/ and backend/ folders.

Complete.projectStructure: Project structure created on your computer

projectTrees.webNextjs:
📁 ~/{projectName}/
├── 📂 src/
│   ├── 📂 domain/          ← Business logic
│   │   ├── models/
│   │   └── services/
│   ├── 📂 ports/            ← Interface definitions
│   ├── 📂 adapters/         ← External integrations
│   │   ├── api/
│   │   └── ui/
│   └── 📂 app/              ← Pages (Next.js)
├── 📄 CLAUDE.md              ← AI architecture rules
└── 📄 package.json
projectTrees.webPython:
📁 ~/{projectName}/
├── 📂 frontend/              ← Next.js (UI)
│   ├── 📂 src/
│   │   ├── domain/ ports/ adapters/
│   │   └── 📂 app/           ← Pages
│   └── 📄 package.json
├── 📂 backend/               ← FastAPI (Server)
│   ├── 📂 domain/
│   │   ├── models/
│   │   └── services/
│   ├── 📂 ports/
│   │   ├── inbound/
│   │   └── outbound/
│   ├── 📂 adapters/
│   │   ├── inbound/api/
│   │   └── outbound/persistence/
│   ├── 📄 main.py            ← Server entry point
│   └── 📂 venv/              ← Python virtual environment
└── 📄 CLAUDE.md               ← AI architecture rules
projectTrees.webJava:
📁 ~/{projectName}/
├── 📂 frontend/              ← Next.js (UI)
│   ├── 📂 src/
│   │   ├── domain/ ports/ adapters/
│   │   └── 📂 app/           ← Pages
│   └── 📄 package.json
├── 📂 backend/               ← Spring Boot (Server)
│   ├── 📂 src/main/java/.../
│   │   ├── domain/model/     ← Entities
│   │   ├── domain/service/   ← Business logic
│   │   ├── port/in/ out/     ← Interfaces
│   │   └── adapter/in/web/   ← Controllers
│   ├── 📄 application.yml    ← Server config
│   └── 📄 build.gradle       ← Build config
└── 📄 CLAUDE.md               ← AI architecture rules
projectTrees.mobile:
📁 ~/{projectName}/
├── 📂 src/
│   ├── 📂 domain/            ← Business logic
│   │   ├── models/
│   │   └── services/
│   ├── 📂 ports/             ← Interface definitions
│   ├── 📂 adapters/          ← External integrations
│   │   ├── api/
│   │   └── ui/
│   │       ├── screens/      ← Screens
│   │       └── components/   ← Components
├── 📄 App.tsx                 ← App entry point
├── 📄 CLAUDE.md               ← AI architecture rules
└── 📄 package.json
projectTrees.dataAi:
📁 ~/{projectName}/
├── 📂 data/                   ← Data files
├── 📂 notebooks/              ← Jupyter Notebook
├── 📂 src/
│   ├── loaders/               ← Data loading
│   ├── analyzers/             ← Analysis logic
│   └── visualizers/           ← Visualization
├── 📂 venv/                   ← Python virtual environment
└── 📄 CLAUDE.md               ← AI project rules

firstPrompt.subtitle: It doesn't have to be perfect. You can keep the conversation going and refine as you go.
firstPrompt.exampleLabel: Here's what it could look like

promptExamples.webNextjs:
I want to build a group event scheduling website.

What it does:
It's a pain when someone asks "when are you free?" in a group chat.
I want a site where you share a link and everyone votes on which dates work for them.

Who it's for:
Young professionals in their 20s–30s. No sign-up required — just open the link and vote.

3 must-have features to start with:
1. Create an event with multiple date options and let participants check the ones that work
2. No sign-up — just enter a name and vote right away
3. A clear view of voting results at a glance

Where should I start?
promptExamples.webPython:
I want to build a neighborhood tool-sharing platform.

What it does:
People in the same apartment complex can lend and borrow things they rarely use,
like drills, ladders, and camping gear.

Who it's for:
Residents in the same building. They don't want to buy stuff they'll only use once.

3 must-have features to start with:
1. List an item to lend (photo + description + availability dates)
2. Search for items and request to borrow them
3. Push notification when someone sends a borrow request

Where should I start?
promptExamples.webJava:
I want to build a staff scheduling website for a small cafe.

What it does:
For cafes with fewer than 10 employees — the owner posts shifts and
staff can claim them. No more coordinating everything through group chats.

Who it's for:
Cafe owners who are tired of scheduling through messaging apps.

3 must-have features to start with:
1. Owner posts available shifts and employees sign up
2. Monthly calendar view to see the full schedule at a glance
3. Notifications when a shift is confirmed

Where should I start?
promptExamples.mobile:
I want to build a water intake tracker app.

What it does:
Track how much water you drink each day with a single tap,
and get a little encouragement when you hit your daily goal.

Who it's for:
People in their 20s–40s starting to build healthier habits.
They want something simple — just log and go.

3 must-have features to start with:
1. One-tap logging (e.g., +250ml per tap)
2. Today's total shown as a number and visual progress bar
3. A congratulatory message when the daily goal is reached

Where should I start?
promptExamples.dataAi:
I want to analyze e-commerce sales data.

What kind of analysis:
I have order data piling up in spreadsheets and I want to figure out
which products sell best and what times of day get the most orders.

How the results will be used:
For a monthly report to present to management.

3 things to analyze first:
1. Monthly sales rankings by product
2. Order patterns by day of week and time of day
3. Identify days with sudden spikes or drops in sales

Where should I start?

followUp.heading: What to Say Next
followUp.subtitle: After your first prompt, the AI will generate code. From there, just chat naturally with short requests.
examples: Can you run this for me?
I want to add a login feature
I'm getting an error, can you help? (paste the error message)
Explain what we've built so far
followUp.reassurance: Don't worry about saying the wrong thing. You can always say "Undo that and try again."

v0Design.heading: Stuck on the Design?
v0Design.subtitle: Use v0.dev to create a UI with AI first, then bring it into your project.
steps: Go to v0.dev and describe the screen you want, like "Create a login page"
If you like it, click Download ZIP to download it
Extract the ZIP and place the files in the {v0Path} folder
Tell Claude Code: "Apply this code to my project"

phase2.heading: Phase 2: Launch Your Service to the World
phase2.description: We'll guide you through database setup, file storage, deploying to a real URL, and configuring AI team agents.

1.title: 1. Information We Collect
1.intro: VibeStart automatically collects the following information to provide its services.
items: Access logs (IP address, access time, browser type)
Service usage records (selected OS, goal, progress steps)
1.note: VibeStart does not require account registration and does not directly collect personally identifiable information such as names or email addresses.

2.title: 2. Purpose of Collection and Use
items: Service usage analytics and quality improvement

3.title: 3. Retention Period
3.content: Collected information is retained for one (1) year from the date of collection and is promptly destroyed thereafter.

4.title: 4. Disclosure to Third Parties
4.intro: VibeStart does not share collected information with third parties, except in the following cases:
items: When required by applicable law or regulation
When the user has given prior consent

5.title: 5. Cookies and Analytics Tools
5.content: VibeStart may use web analytics tools such as Google Analytics for service improvement, which may involve the use of cookies. You may refuse cookie storage through your browser settings; however, some features of the service may be limited as a result.

6.title: 6. Your Rights
6.content: You may request access to, correction of, or deletion of your collected information at any time. Please contact us using the information below.

8.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
8.content: This privacy policy may be updated to reflect changes in applicable laws, policies, or services. Any changes will be posted on this page.
